bullets.text: The World Health Organization has stated that the hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship is not expected to cause a pandemic, as the virus spreads differently from COVID-19. Two Britons evacuated from the affected ship are reportedly improving in hospital.

Germany's Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil has attributed the country's economic slowdown to President Trump's "irresponsible war in Iran." This comes as Trump has given the EU a July 4th ultimatum to approve a trade deal and eliminate tariffs on American goods.

For the first time in nearly two decades, Russia's upcoming Victory Day parade will not feature military hardware, a sign that the Ukraine war may not be proceeding as planned. This absence suggests a potential strain on Russia's military resources.

The federal government will contribute an additional $4 billion to Victoria's Suburban Rail Loop, bringing its total contribution to $6 billion, though this is less than the state government requested.
The Labor Party's decision not to field a candidate in the Farrer by-election has opened the door for One Nation and an independent candidate, potentially redistributing traditional Labor votes.
Two additional women have been arrested and are expected to be charged after arriving back in Australia from Syria with family links to Islamic State.
The US and Iran are reviewing a Washington-backed proposal to end the ongoing conflict, which could stabilize oil prices around $100.
Right-wing nationalists in the US are critical of President Trump's Iran policy, viewing it as a betrayal of his 'America First' pledge.

Saudi Arabia has lifted a suspension on 'Project Freedom' after a call between Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman and the US President, suggesting a potential shift in regional dynamics related to the conflict.
